text.title: Auto-Draft Postmortem, No Auto-Publish Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for inc-postmortem-auto-draft-no-publish. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Agent MUST emit a first draft within 2 hours of incident close; later drafts decay in fidelity as memories fade.
rule.rationale: Allspaw on postmortem cadence — 2h window captures fresh signal; >24h drafts re-litigate.

rule.statement: Every claim in the draft MUST carry an `evidence_url`; un-evidenced sentences are stripped before draft emission.
rule.rationale: SRE workbook — evidence-anchored postmortems hold up under audit.

rule.statement: Contributing factors MUST be marked `candidate=true` until a human reviewer accepts; the agent does NOT finalise factor selection.
rule.rationale: Allspaw / Etsy 2012 — single-author factor selection biases toward narrative; humans must converge.

rule.statement: Agent MUST propose action items as placeholders (no owner, no deadline); humans assign before publication.
rule.rationale: Action items require organisational context the agent lacks; assigning agent-defined owners breaks accountability.

rule.statement: Output MUST carry `published=false` and `signoff_by=null`; only a named human reviewer can set `published=true`.
rule.rationale: Human-in-the-loop policy + audit — auto-published postmortems are the cardinal sin of AI ops.

rule.statement: Agent MUST flag blame-language candidates (e.g. 'should have') but MUST NOT rewrite them; humans control language.
rule.rationale: Language ownership stays with the team; AI rewrite removes contributor agency.

rule.statement: Draft MUST conform to the team's postmortem template structure (sections, headings); deviations rejected.
rule.rationale: Template stability is the reader's only mental model anchor.

rule.statement: If incident artifacts are unstructured or team has no postmortem culture, return `skipped`.
